task.task_description: This is a detector design and performance characterization task for high-energy physics instrumentation. The user needs to design a compact hadronic calorimeter specifically for a mid-energy collider experiment, then simulate its performance using Geant4 Monte Carlo simulations. The task involves exploring different detector geometries and materials, running hadronic shower simulations with charged pions or protons, and measuring key performance metrics including energy resolution, response linearity, and shower containment across multiple energy points. This is a multi-physics engineering problem combining detector design principles with computational simulation and performance analysis.
task.goal: Design and optimize a compact hadronic calorimeter for measuring hadronic particle energies at a mid-energy collider experiment

constraints.description: Simplified experimental environment with no magnetic field
Energy sweep capped at 50 GeV maximum
Limited to 3 energy points for simulation tractability
Must use pi+ or protons in Geant4 particle gun
Hadronic showers at 100+ GeV produce billions of hits (computational limit)

parameters.topology_options: box, cylinder_barrel, projective_tower, forward_disk, shashlik, accordion, segmented, tracking_detector, homogeneous_slab
parameters.measurements_required: energy resolution, response linearity, shower containment

task.context:
Design a compact hadronic calorimeter for measuring hadronic particle energies at a mid-energy collider experiment.

You may assume a simplified experimental environment with no magnetic field.

**GEOMETRY EXPLORATION:**
The geometry tool supports topology parameter: "box", "cylinder_barrel", "projective_tower", "forward_disk", "shashlik", "accordion", "segmented", "tracking_detector", "homogeneous_slab"

PRACTICAL CONSTRAINTS (for tractable simulation):
- Energy sweep capped at 50 GeV and 3 points (hadronic showers at 100+ GeV produce billions of hits)
- Use particle_energy: [10, 30, 50] to run energies in parallel on GPU

Simulate hadronic showers and MEASURE:
- Energy resolution at each energy point
- Response linearity
- Shower containment

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Use pi+ or protons in Geant4 particle gun
- Test at most 3 configurations exploring topology and/or material choices

steps.name: design_baseline_configuration
steps.description: Design baseline sampling calorimeter configuration with steel absorber and plastic scintillator, calculating optimal layer thicknesses and total depth for 95% shower containment at 50 GeV

steps.name: generate_baseline_geometry
steps.description: Generate GDML file for baseline segmented sampling calorimeter with calculated parameters

steps.name: simulate_baseline_energy_sweep
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation with pi+ particles at 10, 30, 50 GeV to characterize baseline detector response

steps.name: design_projective_configuration
steps.description: Design projective tower geometry with lead absorber for improved hermeticity and energy resolution

steps.name: generate_projective_geometry
steps.description: Generate GDML file for projective tower calorimeter with lead-scintillator sampling

steps.name: simulate_projective_energy_sweep
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation of projective tower design with pi+ at 10, 30, 50 GeV

steps.name: design_compensating_configuration
steps.description: Design compensating calorimeter with optimized steel-scintillator ratio to achieve e/h ≈ 1 for improved linearity

steps.name: generate_compensating_geometry
steps.description: Generate GDML file for compensating calorimeter with optimized sampling fraction

steps.name: simulate_compensating_energy_sweep
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation of compensating design with pi+ at 10, 30, 50 GeV

steps.name: analyze_performance_metrics
steps.description: Analyze all simulation results to extract energy resolution, response linearity, and shower containment for each configuration at all energy points

steps.name: create_performance_plots
steps.description: Create ROOT plots showing energy resolution vs energy, response linearity, and shower profiles for all three configurations

steps.name: generate_optimization_report
steps.description: Synthesize all results into final report recommending optimal hadronic calorimeter design based on resolution, linearity, containment, and practical considerations

plan.reasoning: This workflow systematically explores three distinct calorimeter designs: baseline segmented steel-scintillator, projective tower with lead for hermeticity, and compensating design for improved linearity. Energy sweeps run in parallel for efficiency. Analysis extracts all required metrics (resolution, linearity, containment) and produces comprehensive comparison for optimal design selection
